metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `process_signature` in `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` must be updated to pass `kind` through into `state["signature"]`; if the hint is meant to be readable from reduced state (the normal path for 114f), add `"kind": data.get("kind")` to the whitelist and document this as a required change, not an optional one.
findings.finding: The `process_signature` reducer processor builds `state["signature"]` via an explicit field whitelist that does not include `kind`; any `data["kind"]` written by the two call sites will be silently dropped on reduce, making the hint invisible to every reducer-based consumer (including the 114f routing logic that reads from reduced state).
findings.location: Scope: `src/rebar/signing.py` ('kind passthrough if needed'); Mechanism; `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` lines 387-398
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"kind": data.get("kind")` to the `state["signature"]` dict in `process_signature`, and promote the Scope entry from 'if needed' to a definite AC or Scope line, so the plan is complete.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `'kind': data.get('kind')` to the `process_signature` reducer so that `data['kind']` survives compaction into `state['signature']` (or explicitly document that kind is intentionally ephemeral and describe how consumers read it from the raw event instead).
findings.finding: The reducer's process_signature (which is the compaction boundary) enumerates a fixed set of fields to persist into state['signature'] and does not include 'kind'. If kind is written to the SIGNATURE event data but not projected by the reducer, it disappears after compaction — making the routing hint invisible on compacted tickets.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify exactly how `kind` is passed into `sign_manifest` (e.g., add a `kind: str | None = None` kwarg that is merged into `record` before `append_event`) so ACs 1 and 2 have an unambiguous implementation target.
findings.finding: sign_manifest() has no 'kind' parameter today; the plan lists 'signing.py (kind passthrough if needed)' but never specifies the API extension — whether sign_manifest gains a kind= kwarg, callers post-inject it into the record before append_event, or some other mechanism. An executor implementing this story has no unambiguous call-site contract.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `kind: str | None = None` to `sign_manifest`'s signature; include `"kind": kind` in the `record` dict (only when non-None) before the `append_event` call. Call sites pass `kind="plan-review"` or `kind="completion-verifier"` respectively.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the routing-hint-vs-manifest[0]-authority contract (including the '114f trusts kind only when it matches manifest-derived kind' rule) in a CLAUDE.md entry, ADR, or inline docstring in signing.py so it survives beyond the story text.
findings.finding: The routing-hint-vs-authoritative-manifest[0] distinction is a novel architectural contract (kind field is a hint only; 114f trusts it only when it matches the manifest-derived kind) that is load-bearing for any future routing code but is only documented inline in the story text — no ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design-doc update is planned.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a module-level docstring to signing.py (or a CLAUDE.md section under 'Signing contracts') that states: 'data["kind"] is a non-authoritative routing hint. Consumers MUST derive the canonical kind from manifest[0] and use data["kind"] only as a secondary check when it agrees.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add test coverage for: (a) plan-review and completion SIGNATURE events carry the correct `data["kind"]` value, (b) a completion manifest missing the fingerprint is read back as `material-unknown` (the legacy-sig edge path), (c) old signatures remain valid after the change (backward-compat regression test), and (d) drift-refresh re-sign preserves `data["kind"]`.
findings.finding: The plan introduces several new behavioral paths (kind stamping at two call sites, material fingerprint in completion manifest, legacy-sig-missing-fingerprint treated as material-unknown, drift-refresh preserving kind) but describes zero test work — no test files, no AC referencing test coverage, no mention of updating or adding tests. The legacy-completion-sig path (material-unknown) and the backward-compatibility guarantee (no broken old signatures) are user-facing behaviors with no test coverage described at all.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all ACs); Scope section (attest.py, transition.py, signing.py)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a test section or test ACs, e.g.: 'A unit test asserts `sign_plan_review` produces a SIGNATURE event with `data["kind"]=="plan-review"`; a unit test asserts the completion signer produces `data["kind"]=="completion-verifier"` and the fingerprint line; a parametrised test feeds a manifest row without a fingerprint to validity-on-read and asserts it returns material-unknown; a regression test re-verifies a fixture signature captured before this change and asserts it still passes; a test re-signs via `refresh_attestation` and asserts kind is preserved.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether validity-on-read already treats a missing fingerprint as 'material-unknown' (no code change needed) or whether a change to that component is required — and if the latter, add it to the Scope section.
findings.finding: AC3's parenthetical — 'legacy completion sigs without [fingerprint] are treated as material-unknown by validity-on-read' — describes a behavioral requirement for validity-on-read, but validity-on-read is absent from the Scope section. An implementer cannot tell whether this is documenting existing behavior (no action needed) or specifying a new behavior that requires code changes outside the stated scope.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC3 (completion manifest fingerprint) vs. Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to AC3 or the Clarification section: 'validity-on-read already returns material-unknown when the fingerprint line is absent; no change to that component is required by this story.' If that is not yet true, add the relevant file to Scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies the completion signer computes the material fingerprint using the same shared helper as plan-review (no duplicated field-list definition), to cover the 'Added (review)' deliverable.
findings.finding: The 'Added (review)' section describes a concrete, verifiable deliverable — the completion signer must use the SAME helper as plan-review to compute the material fingerprint, rather than redefining the field list — but no Acceptance Criterion covers this constraint, leaving it unverifiable at review time.
findings.suggested_fix: AC6: The material-fingerprint field set (description/AC/file_impact/children) is defined in exactly one location; both plan-review and completion signers invoke the same helper to compute it (no local redefinition in transition.py).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `_verdict_manifest` should call `current_material_fingerprint(ticket_id)` (the convenience wrapper already in attest.py) or construct a `PlanContext` and call `material_fingerprint` directly, and specify the failure behavior when the fingerprint cannot be computed (fail-closed vs. omit the line).
findings.finding: AC3 requires the completion signer to record the material fingerprint using the SAME helper plan-review uses (material_fingerprint(ctx: PlanContext)). The current _verdict_manifest receives only a verify_completion result dict; the plan never specifies how a PlanContext is constructed (or whether current_material_fingerprint is called instead), leaving implementers to guess the construction path.
findings.suggested_fix: Use `current_material_fingerprint(ticket_id, repo_root=repo_root)` — it already exists in attest.py, already uses the same field set, and its None-on-error return is safe here since a missing fingerprint line triggers 'material-unknown' on validity-on-read (as stated in AC3).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add a `kind_from_signature(record: dict) -> str | None` helper (in attest.py or signing.py) that encodes the 'use hint iff it matches manifest-derived kind' rule, or explicitly note that this decision rule is out of scope for this story and will be specified in 114f.
findings.finding: The Clarification references a consumer '114f' that 'uses the hint only iff it matches the manifest-derived kind' — this is the only place the routing-hint decision rule is described, but '114f' does not exist anywhere in the codebase and the rule is not co-located with any schema or function. The processing protocol for consuming data['kind'] is defined nowhere except this plan's prose.

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Broaden the plan title (or add a subtitle) to reflect both deliverables, e.g. 'Signing call sites: stamp data[kind] and record material fingerprint on SIGNATURE events', so the title faithfully covers all non-deferred goals.
findings.finding: The plan title promises only 'stamp data[kind] on SIGNATURE events', but the body also introduces a second distinct behaviour — recording the material fingerprint as a completion manifest line (AC3, 'Added (review)' section). This fingerprint recording is non-trivial additional scope not signalled by the title, creating a minor intent-fidelity gap that could cause the implementing agent to under-scope the work if relying on the title alone.
findings.suggested_fix: Rename to: 'Signing call sites: stamp data[kind] and record completion material fingerprint on SIGNATURE events'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
